note: impl gate: twenty-four of twenty-five frozen scenarios pass under a cold judge re-deriving each oracle independently. Three implementation gaps the added scenarios exposed were closed during deliver, and no pre-existing scenario regressed; the improve-path regression risk was probed directly and held.

the implementation clause added for the atomicity pair stated its split and no-split branches against the same predicate, leaving the no-split branch unreachable. Blind runs split the rule two times in three, citing that clause as justification.

the failing scenario's own fixture is independently falsifiable, so the criterion it defends says to split it while the scenario says not to. This is a contract defect rather than an implementation defect, so no amount of implementation work can honestly clear the gate. Escalating to the owner: the fix requires reopening a scenario this mission froze, which is the one transition the conductor does not take unattended.

owner ratified an Oracle-lens revert on the failing scenario: it is unfrozen and returns to draft so its fixture can be replaced with one that is genuinely inseparable. The scenario was frozen by this same mission, so the reopened contract is this mission's own and no downstream consumer depended on it. Owner also directed that the fail-open eval-layer finding be investigated before this node closes, rather than deferred.

the reopened scenario was rebuilt on a qualifying clause whose second half loses its referent when the first fails, and a third held-out fixture was added after a judge showed the pair was passed by an object-identity proxy. The rebuilt cluster now runs stable across isolated blind agents where the previous fixture flapped.

impl gate re-run: twenty-five of twenty-six pass. Every scenario this change request added passes, no pre-existing scenario regressed, and the held-out generalization case passes unanimously. The single failure is a routing overlap between two sibling skills that predates this work and that this diff does not touch; the earlier gate had passed it by inspection rather than by blind routing runs, so that pass was false.
